events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: snf-critique-finished-product

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request. Treat it as a skill you may invoke if appropriate.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a HALF-BAKED, EARLY-STAGE, UNVALIDATED software/product IDEA and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it BEFORE designing or building.
WHEN NOT TO USE: the product already exists / is live / shipped, and the user wants a review or critique of the EXISTING product (that is product review/audit, not early-stage idea strengthening).
If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message, rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief and hand off. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
Here's my live, shipped product at acme.com with 2,000 users. Can you review it and tell me what's wrong with the current product?

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion interview skill.

The task is: A user has a live, shipped product at acme.com with 2,000 users and wants a review/critique of the existing product.

The skill's WHEN NOT TO USE clause explicitly states: "the product already exists / is live / shipped, and the user wants a review or critique of the EXISTING product (that is product review/audit, not early-stage idea strengthening)."

This case matches the WHEN NOT TO USE condition exactly. The product is live, shipped, has 2,000 real users, and the user wants to know what's wrong with the current product. I should NOT apply the bake-to-completion skill. I should respond as a competent generalist reviewer would.

Adversarial check: Could I argue the skill still applies? No — the skill's own exclusion clause is unambiguous and directly on point. Applying it here would be a misuse of the skill and would irritate a user who wants actionable product feedback, not a Socratic ideation interview about a product that already exists and is live.

My response should acknowledge the request and explain what I'd need to do a meaningful product review, since I can't actually visit acme.com (a placeholder domain used in the prompt rather than a real URL).

```json
{
  "caseId": "snf-critique-finished-product",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Thanks for sharing the context. Since your product is already live and serving 2,000 users, what you need is a product review or audit — not an early-stage ideation process.\n\nTo give you a genuinely useful critique of what's wrong with the current product, I'll need a bit more to work with, since I can't browse acme.com directly in this session. Please share one or more of the following:\n\n1. A description of what the product does and who it serves\n2. Screenshots, a Loom walkthrough, or a design file (Figma, etc.)\n3. Key user flows or the core problem it solves\n4. Any existing feedback signals you have — churn reasons, support tickets, NPS comments, user interviews\n5. Your current biggest concerns or hypotheses about what's broken\n\nWith that, I can give you a structured critique covering areas like: value proposition clarity, onboarding friction, core loop quality, retention risks, UX/UI issues, and any obvious gaps relative to user expectations.\n\nWhat can you share?",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": false,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Read the skill's WHEN NOT TO USE clause",
      "Confirmed the user's product is live, shipped, and has real users — matching the exclusion exactly",
      "Did not apply the bake-to-completion Socratic interview process",
      "Responded as a competent generalist product reviewer, asking for the artifacts needed to do a real audit"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "acme.com is a placeholder and not a real browsable URL in this context",
      "The user genuinely wants actionable critique of the existing product, not ideation support"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Cannot actually visit or audit acme.com without more information from the user",
      "No product details provided, so the response is necessarily a request for more context rather than a substantive critique"
    ]
  }
}
```
